mnemonics.title: Invertible Conditions
mnemonics.content: I2O2: Invertible if and only if (Iff) One-to-One and Onto - for square transformations, one implies the other!

mnemonics.title: Rank-Nullity
mnemonics.content: Rank + Nullity = Domain Dimension. Think: 'RANk NULLity to the DOMAIN' - they always add up!

mnemonics.title: Determinant Test
mnemonics.content: det ≠ 0 means GO ( invertible), det = 0 means STOP (not invertible)

mnemonics.title: Inverse of Product
mnemonics.content: KEOPS: Keep Ends, Open Middle, Switch (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹

acronyms.expansion: Invertible if and only if One-to-One and Onto
acronyms.usage: Condition for linear transformation invertibility in equal-dimension spaces

acronyms.expansion: Keep Ends, Open Middle, Switch order when inverting product

visualTips.visualization: One-to-one: no two different inputs give same output (each output has unique input). Onto: every element in codomain is hit (no misses in output space)
Kernel shows what gets squished to zero - larger kernel = more collapse = less likely invertible
det(A) tells how much area/volume is scaled - det=0 means collapse to zero area (non-invertible)

analogies.analogy: Like a reversible door - you can go forward and backward. A projection is like a one-way mirror (can't go back).
The 'lost information' department - vectors that get crushed to zero can't be recovered.
Rank is the 'effective dimension' of output - like useful information after transformation.
Just like reciprocal for numbers: A⁻¹ 'undoes' A, just like 1/3 undoes multiplying by 3.
